blocks.content: She had to go. It came up the way it always did — her bus, the time, a phone notification she glanced at and didn't react to but it still meant something.
She stood up. Adjusted her bag strap. And then she did the thing she'd been doing more of lately — she didn't rush the goodbye.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I'm really glad I asked you to the movies.
She was on the step below me now, which put our faces close to even. The city noise was back — a horn somewhere, a bus door hissing. But right here on the steps it was quiet.
She looked at me for a moment. Longer than a goodbye look.

blocks.prompt: She's right here. The steps are empty. She's looking at you like she's waiting for you to decide something.

options.label: I lean forward. Slowly. I let her close it if she wants to.
I almost do. Something stops me. I say 'same time next week?' instead.
"I really like you." I say it before I can make it smaller.
